auth_type: Custom (API Key, Bearer Token, or OAuth2)
auth_notes: Authentication depends entirely on the implementation of the specific Node.js application. Common patterns include JWT via Passport.js, static API keys in headers, or Basic Auth.
rate_limits: Determined by the host environment or middleware (e.g., express-rate-limit). No default global limit.
gotchas: Payload size limits in Express/Body-parser can cause 413 errors for large n8n data transfers
CORS configuration must explicitly allow the n8n instance domain if using browser-based triggers
Asynchronous execution in the Node.js app may require a polling mechanism or callback webhook if the process takes longer than the HTTP timeout
client_must_provide: Base URL of the deployed Node.js application
Authentication credentials (API Key, Client Secret, etc.)
JSON Schema or OpenAPI/Swagger documentation for endpoints
research_notes: Integration requires using the HTTP Request node to interface with the Node.js REST/GraphQL API. If the goal is simply to run Node.js code within the workflow, the native n8n 'Code' node should be used instead of an external integration.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Integration requires using the HTTP Request node to interface with the Node.js REST/GraphQL API. If the goal is simply to run Node.js code within the workflow, the native n8n 'Code' node should be used instead of an external integration.
